profile.source: context/PROJECT_STATUS.json and game skill profile; arc-profile registry fallback rejected as unmatched

gameLoopReview.evidence: No context/GAME_LOOP.md or recent docs/PLAYTESTS files; scores derive from live browser/runtime systems and existing regression surfaces.

items.title: Make Tell the Commissioner reliably open and expose failures
items.premise: openFeedback awaits optional clipboard work before window.open, losing trusted click activation; both mounted click handlers swallow rejected promises.

L1.recipe: Open a placeholder window synchronously and navigate it after context assembly.

L2.recipe: Add injectable window/clipboard adapters, blocked-popup fallback navigation, visible error reporting, and focused tests.

L3.recipe: Also validate the complete Settings and Franchise Moment interaction paths in Playwright.

items.title: Ship potential-aware, injury-restoring live snap allocation
items.premise: QB defaults below 100%; automatic room shares are package/order based and do not incorporate player potential; injured players leave the active roster but the restoration contract is untested.

L1.recipe: Lock healthy QB1/K1/P1 to all role snaps and test it.

L2.recipe: Blend overall, potential, fit, morale, and depth intent into automatic room shares; exclude injured players and prove restoration after recovery while preserving manual overrides.

L3.recipe: Add UI explanations, live effective-share visualization, and multi-week distribution realism checks.

items.title: Expand box scores and every player-stat lens with truthful football telemetry
items.premise: Season/career schemas already track rich fields, but box-score groups omit many fields, punting, blocking, and snaps; team third-down values are estimated rather than observed.

L1.recipe: Expose already-tracked player fields and add punting.

L2.recipe: Carry snap/blocking deltas into game stats, add efficiency columns, and derive down/distance conversion metrics from stamped plays rather than estimates.

L3.recipe: Add a broadcast-style game strip, quarter differential, efficiency tiles, and game-impact leaders.

items.title: Generate personalized bios, facts, achievements, and career identity for every player
items.premise: Player profiles expose ratings, contract, history, and a generic hero, but no deterministic narrative synthesizes position, age, overall, potential, attributes, stats, awards, health, and career context.

L1.recipe: Create a deterministic source-derived bio and fact list.

L2.recipe: Add position-specific identity, trajectory, signature attributes, truthful achievements, milestone facts, and dossier visuals for all players.

L3.recipe: Add evolving career chapters, game-ball moments, and profile share cards.

verification: narrative unit tests across every position

items.title: Place potential beside overall on every player surface
items.premise: Potential is persisted and visible in the profile hero but omitted from roster, free agency, retired, contract, draft, history, commissioner, and statistical row builders.

L1.recipe: Add POT immediately after OVR in principal roster/draft/contract tables.

L2.recipe: Cover every player-row builder and stat table, plus consistent labels and potential-gap tone.

L3.recipe: Add dual-rating visual meters and development-window explanations.

items.title: Prove every button and delegated action has a working contract
items.premise: Existing browser-wiring tests cover selected paths but there is no exhaustive static action inventory, and several UI handlers intentionally swallow errors.

L1.recipe: Inventory button IDs/data-action contracts and fail on orphaned controls.

L2.recipe: Add static contract validation plus focused runtime/Playwright coverage for high-value actions and visible error paths.

L3.recipe: Add state-aware interaction crawling across all tabs with allowlisted destructive actions.

preverifiedSkips.reason: Rejected: a persisted seeded potential rating already exists; the real gap is visibility and rotation use.
Rejected: rich PFR-inspired tables already exist; the real gap is box-score parity and universal rating context.
Rejected as phrased: injured players are already excluded by getTeamPlayers; the missing contract is merit rebalance and restoration proof.
Deferred honestly: real on-domain email receipt and live-origin evidence remain external proof gates.
